arguments.task: Open and read the strongest official Google Ads developer pages needed to determine: one-line product/API description, documented authentication methods, access or gating requirements, API surface type and breadth, rate limit notes, and whether official docs mention an MCP/server. Prefer canonical first-party pages such as developers.google.com pages for overview, OAuth/auth, REST/reference, quotas/rate limits, and access levels or getting-started pages. Return the final URLs actually read and concise extracted findings with quotes or paraphrases tied to each page. Do not rely on search-result snippets.

input.thought: Start a focused browser run on official Google Ads developer documentation to extract evidence-backed product, auth, access, API surface, and rate-limit details.

raw_result.description: Programmatic interface for managing Google Ads accounts, campaigns, ads, assets, and reporting at scale.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth 2.0 for API authorization and require a developer token in request headers for API access. OAuth docs cover installed app, web app, and service account flows; the developer token is a Google Ads-specific requirement rather than a standard API key/bearer token scheme.
raw_result.gating_reason: Beyond creating a Google Ads manager account and project, production use requires a developer token and an assigned access level; quota docs explicitly tie operation limits to access levels such as Explorer and Basic, indicating access is subject to approval rather than open self-serve production access.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs show a broad Google Ads API covering core advertising resources and operations such as campaigns, ads, assets, customer/account management, and reporting/search. Evidence gathered also indicates JSON REST usage, but the browser notes mention gRPC as well and I could not directly verify the canonical transport page from the captured output.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Google Ads developer page gathered in this pass mentions an MCP server or Model Context Protocol offering.

raw_result.blocker: Toolkit development is feasible with public docs, but real production use requires obtaining and configuring a Google Ads developer token/access level in addition to OAuth credentials.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official quota docs indicate limits vary by access level; the gathered evidence notes Explorer production access at 2,880 operations/day and Basic access at 15,000 operations/day, plus a 10,000-operation limit for a single mutate request and a 64 MB gRPC message-size limit.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong first-party developer documentation, explicit OAuth guidance, explicit developer-token requirement, and published quota/access-level concepts indicate a mature API suitable for agent tooling once credentials and token approval are in place.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field api_surface.type is not fully supported by the captured evidence because the browser-extracted notes mention both REST and gRPC, but I did not directly read a canonical official transport/reference page in the gathered output to verify whether the schema should be REST or Both.

result.gating_reason: Beyond creating a Google Ads manager account and project, production use requires a developer token and an assigned access level; quota docs explicitly tie operation limits to access levels such as Explorer and Basic, indicating access is subject to approval rather than open self-serve production access.

Evidence gathered also indicates JSON REST usage, but the browser notes mention gRPC as well and I could not directly verify the canonical transport page from the captured output.
